articles.title: These AI startups are growing revenue at faster and faster rates
articles.description: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.

articles.title: Solo GP Ashley Smith announces second $25M fund to back startups in AI, security and more
articles.description: Vermilion Cliffs Ventures announced Wednesday the close of a $25 million Fund II.

articles.title: Apple to produce Made in America wireless chips with Broadcom
articles.description: Apple has signed a $30 billion+ multiyear deal with Broadcom to design and produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made custom wireless connectivity chips for Apple products.

articles.title: Venus Aerospace raises $90M Series B to build a new kind of rocket engine
articles.description: Build a new kind of rocket engine and the world will beat a path to your door.

articles.title: Blue Origin reportedly raising $10B at $130B valuation
articles.description: Blue Origin is raising $10 billion at a $130 billion pre-money valuation from Coatue Asset Management, Bezos himself, and other big-name investors, The New York Times reported.

articles.title: Venus Williams-backed WeWard can now lock your apps until you hit your steps
articles.description: With funding from tennis star and angel investor Venus Williams, the French app WeWard says that it increases walking time by almost 25%.

articles.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
articles.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

articles.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
articles.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

articles.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
articles.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11 billion valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

articles.title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
articles.description: If you're building something ambitious, this is a fast track to the people who can move your startup forward.

articles.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
articles.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

articles.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
articles.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

articles.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
articles.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

articles.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
articles.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

articles.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
articles.description: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

articles.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
articles.description: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

articles.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
articles.description: Netflix is bringing 2- to 20-minute videos to its platform through new partnerships with digital publishers, including Rolling Stone and Variety.

articles.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
articles.description: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

articles.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
articles.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

articles.title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
articles.description: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.

articles.title: US rare earths flow to Asia as domestic demand is slow to emerge
articles.description: Miners backed by Trump admin. sell to Japan, South Korea despite push to develop domestic supply chain.

articles.title: Blue Origin, for the first time, is expected to raise private capital
articles.description: The company is raising $10 billion, leading to a valuation of $130 billion.

articles.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
articles.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."

articles.title: Michigan sees explosive outbreak of diarrheal parasite with over 700 cases
articles.description: Cases have risen quickly as officials are working to identify a common source.

articles.title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
articles.description: Squeeze on Rust Belt electricity bills threatens Trump’s manufacturing plan.

articles.title: Surprisingly large number of people may have marker for tick-linked meat allergy
articles.description: There's still a slew of questions about why some people develop alpha-gal syndrome.

articles.title: SCOTUS lets Texas enforce app store law that Big Tech calls "censorship regime"
articles.description: Texas win at 5th Circuit left in place as attempts to overturn age law continue.

articles.title: Bethesda, id Software reportedly hit hard by Microsoft layoffs
articles.description: As much as 50 percent of some teams affected by reductions, and more could be coming.

articles.title: Google's Pixel 11 launch event is set for August 12, with possible price increases
articles.description: Google's new phones could feature glowing LEDs and higher price tags.

articles.title: The Weather Channel increases streaming subscription prices by up to $20
articles.description: Livestreaming the channel through its app now starts at $5 per month.

articles.title: The Nintendo Switch's days are numbered—but what is that number?
articles.description: Ars analysis suggests the 9-year-old console could keep selling for years.

articles.title: This race car is made from plant fibers, volcanoes, ... and seawater?
articles.description: The T70S can be eligible for racing events or built to be road-legal.

articles.title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
articles.description: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.

articles.title: Dragonflies maneuver like fighter pilots
articles.description: Male dragonflies' dramatic aerial combat maneuvers emerge from relatively simple vision-based rules.

articles.title: New virus catalog reveals which pathogens pose the greatest threat
articles.description: The data can help predict what a future pandemic virus might look like.

articles.title: ULA's last six Atlas Vs can't launch anything besides Boeing's Starliner
articles.description: Amazon says it has enough satellites in orbit to begin initial broadband service at mid-latitudes later this year.

articles.title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
articles.description: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.

articles.title: FCC to end Biden-era rule that forces ISPs to list all their fees
articles.description: FCC to let ISPs stop listing all passthrough fees, give single "up to" price.

articles.title: Kremlin suspected of flying drones over Europe using Russian shadow fleet
articles.description: Drone intruders that possibly flew from Russian ships showed Europe isn’t ready.

articles.title: What is the oldest American object ever launched into space?
articles.description: From a Revolutionary War flag to the Statue of Liberty...

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI初创公司收入增长加速**：多家AI初创企业（如SambaNova）实现高速营收增长，SambaNova在5个月内再次融资10亿美元，估值达110亿美元；同时，法国AI初创公司ZML发布免费产品，旨在加速多芯片推理效率。
2. **重大投融资与估值动态**：Blue Origin据报正以1300亿美元估值融资100亿美元；Venus Aerospace完成9000万美元B轮融资，研发新型火箭发动机；Solo GP Ashley Smith宣布第二支2500万美元基金，专注于AI与安全领域投资。
3. **科技巨头战略调整与产品争议**：苹果与博通合作生产美国制造的无线芯片；Meta推出AI图像生成器Muse Image，但因用户照片使用引发争议；微软为削减成本更依赖自有AI模型；Discord因AI审核漏洞误封用户账号。
4. **高管人事变动与行业趋势**：前OpenAI高管Kevin Weil加入火箭公司Stoke Space董事会；开源AI兴起尚未对Anthropic构成实质威胁，反映出行业竞争格局变化。
5. **活动与产品更新**：Google Pixel发布会定于8月12日；WeWard（由维纳斯·威廉姆斯支持）推出新功能，可锁定应用直至用户完成步数目标；Startup Battlefield澳大利亚申请延期至7月20日。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI初创公司营收加速增长与大规模融资并行，同时科技巨头在芯片自产、AI成本控制及产品伦理方面展开新博弈，行业正进入高速扩张与监管反思并存的关键阶段。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **Hugging Face平台多项更新**：推出原生速度vLLM transformers建模后端、一键部署至Amazon SageMaker Studio、支持在Foundry托管计算上运行模型，并与SkyPilot合作实现零出口费用跨云存储与运行AI工作负载，同时发布LeRobot v0.6.0、PRX数据策略及Kernels重大更新。

2. **Google与OpenAI扩展AI能力**：Gemini API新增托管Agent功能（支持后台任务、远程MCP等）；OpenAI公布澳大利亚支付公司Australian Payments Plus采用ChatGPT与Codex、日本MUFG银行计划转型为AI原生企业等案例。

3. **产业级世界模型与具身智能进展**：魔芯MoWorld实现50FPS、成本降低70%，推动世界模型进入产业应用；具身智能“高考”评测中，人类满分100分，最强模型仅得12.8分，凸显当前AI在复杂任务上的巨大差距。

4. **国内AI与机器人生态活动**：淘天与ATH事业群联合举办“硬核少年技术节”，发布四项AIGX成果；2026世界机器人大会新闻发布会召开，主题为“人机共生，产需共融”。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Hugging Face在模型部署、跨云存储与Agent能力上实现多项底层突破，同时产业级世界模型与具身智能评测结果揭示AI从实验室走向真实场景仍面临严峻挑战。
